items.title: Label inner and outer before differentiating
items.avoid: Jumping straight into derivative notation without identifying u = g(x).
items.prefer: Write u = g(x), y = f(u), then dy/dx = f′(u)·u′.
items.why: Clear decomposition prevents evaluating f′ at the wrong point and mirrors how autograd decomposes graphs.
items.example: For ln(1+x²): u = 1+x², f(u)=ln u → f′(u)·2x = 2x/(1+x²).

items.title: Trace one backward step at a time
items.avoid: Memorizing backprop formulas without connecting them to local derivatives.
items.prefer: For each edge in the graph, write upstream_grad × local_derivative and check tensor shapes.
items.why: Shape-aware chain rule debugging catches most custom-layer gradient bugs before training.
items.example: ReLU backward: upstream gradient multiplied by 1 where z>0, else 0—local derivative of max(0,z).
